text: Chiral nanographenes represent a captivating frontier in polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) research, merging the structural elegance of inherent chirality with the exceptional electronic, optical, and magnetic properties of graphene-like frameworks. These non-planar, contorted architectures—ranging from twisted acenes to complex helicene hybrids—offer a versatile platform for the development of next-generation nanoelectronic and optoelectronic materials. Precise control over their chiroptical properties, such as circular dichroism (CD) and circularly polarized luminescence (CPL), is increasingly achievable through rational synthetic design. This review highlights recent and significant advancements in the synthesis of chiral nanographenes, with a particular emphasis on innovative strategies beyond conventional Scholl chemistry. We explore emerging classes including expanded and $\pi$-extended helicenes, imide-functionalized nanostructures, multiple helicenes, and nonalternant systems containing non-hexagonal rings. By detailing the synthesis, configurational stability, and unique physical behavior of these molecules, this work aims to illustrate how structural contortion and symmetry breaking drive the discovery of novel materials for spintronics and advanced electronics.
